w:r.w:t: AI-Powered Flood Prediction & Infrastructure Cascade Monitoring Platform
For use by the CascadeNet Frontend Engineering Team | Version 1.0

CascadeNet uses a dark, mission-critical aesthetic — designed for high-pressure environments where readability under stress is non-negotiable. Think NASA operations center meets smart city infrastructure. High contrast, minimal decoration, every element earns its place.
Primary action color. Used for interactive elements, active states, highlights, data emphasis, primary CTAs, and the CascadeNet logo.
Hover state for primary blue. Used in animated elements and active map overlays.
Root background. The base of every screen.
RED ALERT — Critical flood warning. Zone in imminent danger. Used for zone fills, alert banners, danger badges. Always animated (pulse).
AMBER WATCH — Single signal elevated. Standby advisory. Used for watch zones, monitoring badges.
GREEN CLEAR — No threat. System operational. Used for safe zones, system health indicators.
Cascade prediction secondary indicators. Infrastructure chain visualizations.
Body text, headings, values in dark contexts.
Timestamps, metadata, placeholder text, deemphasized data.
Used exclusively on the GIS map flood depth layer. Represents water depth from 0m to 3m+:
Ground floor flooding. Hospital risk zone.
Logo, dashboard titles, large metric values, section headers
All numeric data, timestamps, coordinates, alert codes, API values, zone IDs
Descriptions, alert body text, action items, labels
API schemas, config values, JSON previews in spec documents
Never use system fonts (Arial, Inter, Roboto) in the UI — these feel generic and unprofessional for a mission-critical platform.
All numeric values (probabilities, water levels, lead times, coordinates) must use Space Mono — this ensures consistent character width and makes scanning fast.
Dashboard section headers use Syne 700 at 20–24px. Card labels use Space Mono 400 at 9–10px ALL CAPS with 2px letter-spacing.
Line height: 1.6 for body text, 1.2 for display/heading text, 1.0 for monospace data displays.
Component internal padding — tags, chips, small buttons
Card internal padding (small), gap between inline elements
Standard card padding. Gap between cards in a grid.
Section inner padding. Standard grid gap.
Between major sections within a dashboard.
Page-level vertical rhythm. Cover page spacing.

Grid system: 12-column responsive grid with 20px column gap. Dashboard layouts use named grid areas for clarity. Standard breakpoints: 1280px (standard), 1600px (wide), 1920px (ultrawide command center). Mobile (< 768px) shows simplified single-column public portal only — command dashboards require minimum 1280px.
Border default: 1px solid #1A3A5C — used on all cards and panels
Border bright: 1px solid #1E4D7A — used on hover states and focused elements
Border accent: 1px solid #00A8FF — used on active/selected states
Glow blue: 0 0 20px rgba(0,168,255,0.3) — active elements, selected cards
Glow red: 0 0 20px rgba(255,59,92,0.4) — RED alert cards and zones
Glow green: 0 0 15px rgba(0,214,143,0.3) — system health and safe status
Border radius: 0px — CascadeNet uses sharp corners throughout. No rounded corners. This communicates precision and authority.
│ Logo │ Live Status │ Alert Badges │ ░░░░ │ Time │ 🔔 │ User │ ⎋ │
│60px │ Dashboard panels swap here based on sidebar selection │
│ 🏠 │ Each panel owns its own internal grid layout │
[Notification panel slides in from right over main content]
💡 Sidebar icons use tooltips on hover. Active dashboard shown with blue left border + icon glow. No text labels on sidebar — icons only.
Static. Clicking returns to last active dashboard.
7px circle, animated blink, #00D68F when clear / #FF3B5C when active alert
Color reflects highest current alert level system-wide
Animated border badges — e.g. '⬤ 2 RED' '⬤ 1 WATCH'
Click opens notification panel. Badge count updates via WebSocket. Pulse animation active.
Space Mono 11px, IST timezone, updates every second
34×34px button, badge shows unread count in red
Shows logged-in user. Click for role context.
Space Mono 10px, border button, red on hover
Main Command Center (all zones, all alerts)
Data feed status, model version, API uptime
Width: 60px fixed. Never expands. Icon-only navigation.
Active state: Blue left border (3px), icon color #00A8FF, subtle blue background rgba(0,168,255,0.1).
Hover state: Border becomes visible (#1A3A5C), icon lightens to #7AA8CC.
Role filtering: Sidebar items are conditionally rendered based on logged-in user role. A public user never sees the Reservoir Control icon.
Notification badge on bell icon in topnav — not on sidebar items.
NOTIFICATION PANEL (slides in from right, 340px wide)

💡 Notification items are role-filtered. Dam operator only sees reservoir-relevant alerts. NDRF sees deployment notifications. Left border color encodes severity (3px: red/amber/green). Clicking any item navigates to the relevant dashboard with the alert pre-expanded.
│ FLOOD COMMAND PLATFORM │ ← Space Mono 9px, #3D6A8A, 3px letter-spacing
│ [ ACCESS COMMAND CENTER ] │ ← solid blue button
│ Demo credentials shown here │ ← blue left-border info box

💡 Role selection is visual — 2×2 grid of role cards. Selecting a role highlights it with blue border + background. Only one role can be active at once. Each role routes to a different dashboard on login.
Dark #050D1A base with faint blue grid lines (60px grid, rgba(0,168,255,0.04)). Radial glow pulse animation centered.
Grid is static CSS background. Glow uses CSS keyframe scale animation (4s, infinite).
440px wide, #0A1628 background, 1px #1E4D7A border. Box shadow: 0 0 40px rgba(0,168,255,0.15). Slides up on load (translateY animation, 0.5s ease-out).
No rounded corners. Sharp edges convey precision.
Full width, #050D1A background, 1px #1A3A5C border, Space Mono 13px, 11px vertical padding. Focus state: border becomes #00A8FF, 3px blue outer glow.
Type=text for username, type=password for password. Placeholder text in #3D6A8A.
2×2 grid, 8px gap. Each card: #050D1A bg, 1px #1A3A5C border, icon + 2-line label, Space Mono 10px. Hover: border lightens. Active: blue border + blue background rgba(0,168,255,0.1) + blue glow.
One active at a time. Tab-navigable. Required for form submission.
Full width, solid #00A8FF background, black text, Syne 700 13px, 2px letter-spacing ALL CAPS. Hover: lightens to #00E5FF, upward translate, blue glow.
Disabled state (gray) until both fields filled and role selected.
Below button. Left-border blue box (#0A1628 bg, blue left border 2px). Space Mono 9px, muted color. Shows 4 credential pairs.
Developer/demo convenience. Remove or gate behind flag in production.
The primary dashboard. Used by District Collectors and State EOC operators. Designed to give a complete situational picture in under 10 seconds.
│ COMMAND CENTER [LIVE badge] [Last updated: 14 min ago] │
│ ZONES AT │ ACTIVE │ PEAK LEAD │ INFRASTRUCTURE │
│ RED ALERT │ WARNINGS │ TIME │ AT RISK │
│ INTERACTIVE RISK MAP (flex: ~60% of remaining height) │
│ [Zone A: GREEN] [Zone B: RED pulsing] [Zone C: AMBER] │
│ 🟡 Zone A — 54% — 11h 40m │ T+4h: Hospital Y floods │
💡 Bottom row is a 2-column split: alerts feed (scrollable list) and cascade timeline. Both scroll independently. Map takes priority vertical space.
Value turns red with glow when > 0
Min lead_time_hours across all RED zones
Count of cascade nodes with hours_until_failure < 12
Scrollable list of all active alerts ordered by severity then lead time. Each alert card:
Left border color encodes severity (4px: red / amber / green)
Badge shows severity level (RED WARNING / AMBER WATCH)
Title shows zone name + probability + lead time
Body shows primary action item (first item from truth table)
Action chips show department tags (e.g. 'DAM OPS', 'NDRF', 'COLLECTOR')
Clicking expands full action panel inline OR navigates to zone detail
RED alerts have a subtle pulsing red box-shadow to demand attention
RESOLVED alerts shown at bottom in muted style — dismissable
Sequential list showing infrastructure failure events ordered by time. Each row:
Space Mono 10px, #00A8FF, right-aligned, 52px wide — shows '+Xh' format
IBM Plex Sans 600 12px — infrastructure name + event type
IBM Plex Sans 300 11px, #7AA8CC — e.g. '450-bed hospital ground floor floods'
Space Mono 9px chip — which dept must act
1px #1A3A5C connecting dots — gives timeline visual continuity
The map is the most visually impactful component. It must communicate zone risk levels, infrastructure status, water flow direction, and flood depth — all simultaneously. Built on Mapbox GL JS.
│ [Zone A: GREEN fill] ↓ [Flow arrow] ↓ [Zone B: RED fill pulsing]│
│ [Flood depth heat layer — blue gradient over low-elevation] │
💡 Layer toggles at bottom-left. Legend at bottom-right. Coordinates at bottom-center. All three persist on top of the map.
Zones colored by alert level: RED (#FF3B5C, 15% opacity fill, 2px border, pulse animation), AMBER (#FFB340, 12% opacity), GREEN (#00D68F, 8% opacity). Borders always visible.
Gradient from #E3F2FD (0m) → #64B5F6 (0.5m) → #1E88E5 (1m) → #1565C0 (1.5m) → #0D47A1 (2m) → #01579B (3m+). Only renders in amber/red zones.
SVG icons: ⚡ power, 🏥 hospital, 🛣 road, 📡 tower, 💧 water plant. Color: SAFE=#00D68F border, RISK=#FFB340 border (blink), FAIL=#FF3B5C border (fast blink).
Arrows along river paths showing upstream→downstream direction. #00A8FF, 60% opacity, animated downward movement (CSS keyframe).
Dashed green lines (#00D68F) showing pre-computed safe evacuation paths from NDMA data. Width 3px dashed.
When cascade predicted: red animated lines connecting nodes in failure sequence. Animated dash-offset to show propagation direction.
SRTM terrain rendered as subtle hillshade — darker = lower elevation. Helps visualize flood risk topology.
Zone border brightens. Tooltip appears: zone name, alert level, flood probability %, lead time. Cursor: pointer.
Side panel or modal opens with full prediction data, all department actions, cascade timeline for that zone.
Tooltip: node name, type, failure threshold, time to failure (if at risk). Cursor: pointer.
Detail card expands showing: current status, dependencies, cascade impact, responsible department action.
Zones remain visible at all zoom levels. Infrastructure nodes appear at zoom > 10. Street labels at zoom > 13.
Layer fades in/out with 300ms opacity transition. Button shows active state (blue border).
Shows route details: start point, end point, estimated travel time, road name.
Bottom-left: Layer toggle button group — [LIVE] [DEPTH] [NODES] [ROUTES] [CASCADE] [TERRAIN]. Each is a Space Mono 9px chip. Active = blue border + bg.
Bottom-right: Legend panel — semi-transparent dark background, 1px border, compact legend showing zone colors + infrastructure icon meanings.
Bottom-center: Coordinate display — Space Mono 9px, muted color, updates on mouse move.
Top-left: Map status tags — [LIVE], current alert count badge, data freshness timestamp.
Top-right: Mapbox default zoom controls (styled to match dark theme).

A directed dependency graph showing how infrastructure nodes are connected and how failures propagate. Built with D3.js force-directed graph layout. Accessible from the 'Cascade Analysis' sidebar item.
│ Node size = number of dependents. Edge thickness = criticality │
💡 SIMULATE button triggers animated cascade propagation — nodes turn red in sequence with timed delays matching the failure timeline.
Power substations, grid nodes. Yellow — energy/electricity association.
Hospitals, clinics, medical centers. Red — health urgency.
Water treatment, pumping stations. Teal — water association.
Mobile towers, relay stations. Purple — signal/frequency.
Any node predicted to fail within lead time. Amber overrides type color. Slow blink animation.
Any node below failure threshold. Red overrides type color. Fast blink + glow animation.
Nodes confirmed safe at projected water level. Green tint.
When SIMULATE is clicked, the graph animates the failure cascade in sequence:
All nodes start at their safe/at-risk color.
After a 500ms delay, the first node to fail (earliest time) pulses and turns red.
Edges leading to dependent nodes animate (dash-offset animation, red color propagating along edge).
Dependent nodes then turn red in sequence, each with a delay proportional to their actual time difference.
A timeline scrubber at the bottom of the graph shows current simulation time as it progresses.
RESET returns all nodes to initial state.
Use d3.forceSimulation() with forceLink(), forceManyBody(), forceCenter() for automatic layout.
Node radius: proportional to number of dependents (min 16px, max 36px). More dependents = larger node.
Edge thickness: proportional to dependency criticality (1–4px).
Edge color: #1A3A5C default, animates to #FF3B5C during cascade propagation.
Use d3.timer() or sequential setTimeout chains to drive the cascade animation at realistic time proportions.
Allows operators to scrub through the predicted disaster progression hour by hour. Shows how flood extent, infrastructure status, and risk levels evolve over the 6-24 hour prediction window.
│ DISASTER TIMELINE — Zone B ▶ PLAY ⏹ STOP │
│ [Map snapshot — shows flood extent at T+0h] │
│ [Infrastructure nodes show status at this time step] │
│ NOW T+2h T+4h T+6h T+8h T+10h T+12h │
│ ▶ YOU ARE HERE — T+6h — PEAK FLOOD │
💡 The map preview area updates as the scrubber moves. Timeline events are marked with dots on the track — filled dot = event has occurred at current scrubber position, hollow = future.
Full-width horizontal bar, #0A1628 bg, 2px #1A3A5C border, 8px height
Click anywhere on track to jump to that time. Scrubber handle: 16px wide, blue, draggable.
Space Mono 9px labels every 2 hours below track
T+0, T+2h, T+4h... up to prediction horizon (usually T+24h).
Vertical tick marks on track at event times, colored by severity
Hover shows event name tooltip. Click jumps scrubber to that time.
▶ PLAY / ⏸ PAUSE / ⏹ RESET — Space Mono 10px, icon + label
Play animates scrubber in real time. Speed multiplier buttons change animation rate.
400px height snapshot of the zone map at current time step
Updates to reflect flood extent and infrastructure status at scrubber time. Uses static pre-computed snapshots or interpolated opacity layers.
Current time step's events highlighted in alert panel below
Active events shown in full color. Past events grayed. Future events muted.

Each stakeholder role sees a different dashboard when they log in. Same underlying data — completely different layout, emphasis, and action items. The design principle: show only what is needed to make the next decision.
Purpose: Full situational awareness across all zones. Prioritize resources. Coordinate all departments.
│ 🔴 Zone B — 87% — 6h 12m │ T+2h: NH-65 ─ Close road │
│ 🟡 Zone A — 54% — 11h │ T+4h: Hospital Y ─ Backup power │
💡 This is the default view after District Collector login. Shows everything. All departments' action items visible.
Purpose: Monitor reservoir level, receive gate recommendations, understand downstream impact. Minimal interface — only reservoir-relevant data.
│ RESERVOIR GAUGE │ GATE RECOMMEND. │ UPSTREAM RAINFALL │
│ Full: 215 TMC │ GATE 2 + GATE 4 │ Zone A: 84mm/hr ↑↑ │
│ [Zone B: RED 87%] → [Zone C: AMBER 43%] → [Zone D: GREEN 12%] │
│ LAST ACTION: Monitoring only — Updated 14 min ago │

💡 Gate recommendation card is the visually dominant element — large, high contrast, with source citation. Downstream map is simplified (no infrastructure nodes — just zone color codes and evacuation status).
Fixed width 160px × 280px. Vertical fill bar — #050D1A background, fill level represents % capacity.
Smooth CSS height transition when data updates (0.8s ease). Fill color: #00D68F below 70%, #FFB340 70–85%, #FF3B5C above 85%.
Syne 800 28px, centered over gauge, color matches fill color.
Space Mono 10px below gauge: Full / Now / Inflow / Outflow values in separate labeled rows.
Horizontal dashed line at 85% mark on gauge — red dashed, labeled 'ALERT THRESHOLD'.
Large left-border card. 4px left border color matches alert level (red/amber/green). Box-shadow glow matches level.
Syne 700 20px — the gate action. e.g. 'OPEN GATE 2 + GATE 4'. This is the most prominent text on the dashboard.
IBM Plex Sans 300 11px — 'Source: CWC Dam Safety Protocol, Section 6.1'. Italic, muted color. Gives authority.
Space Mono bold — 'ACT WITHIN 6h 12m'. Ticking countdown. Red when < 2 hours remaining.
Small tag showing which confirmation signals are active — RAINFALL ✓ RIVER LEVEL ✓ RESERVOIR ✓
Purpose: Where to deploy, which roads are open, how long before access is cut off. Maximum clarity, minimum clutter. Used on mobile in field conditions.
│ FIELD OPS — Zone B Response 🔴 RED ALERT ACTIVE │
│ ❌ NH-65 — CLOSES 2h │ Eluru Bus Depot Staging Area │

💡 Road status is the most critical info for NDRF — shown as large, colored, immediately scannable. Coordinates have a one-tap copy button for GPS devices. Map is tactical — road overlays prominent, flood depth muted.
Purpose: Tell the public whether they are at risk and what to do. Single page. No technical data. Designed for any device.
│ │ [SIMPLE MAP — Your zone RED + Green route arrow] │ │
│ Relief Camp: Eluru Govt School (2.3 km via NH-30) │
│ [ Enter your area / PIN code to check your zone ] │

💡 No navigation, no technical data, no probabilities. Zone risk shown as a single large colored element. One clear action in large text. Map shows one thing: your area + the route out. Language toggle for regional languages (Telugu/Hindi).
All components are stateless where possible — they receive data as props and render. State is managed at the dashboard level using a central store (React Context or Zustand).
All dashboards (different layer configs per role)
// Zustand or React Context global store
alerts: Alert[], // ordered by severity then lead time
WebSocket connection established on login. Server pushes updates every 15 minutes (or immediately on new alert):
// On ZONE_UPDATE: update zones store → all subscribed components re-render
// On NEW_ALERT: prepend to alerts[] + push to notifications[] + update badge count
// Map zones re-color automatically via derived state from zones store
All GIS map rendering — zone polygons, flood depth raster, infrastructure icons, evacuation routes, flow arrows
Best-in-class WebGL map performance. Supports custom layers, smooth animations, tile-based raster. Free tier sufficient for hackathon.
Cascade dependency graph (force-directed), rainfall bar charts, reservoir gauge fill, timeline track
Industry standard for custom data visualization. Force simulation ideal for dependency graphs.
Upstream rainfall bar chart, historical river level line chart in dashboard cards
React-native, easy to integrate, sufficient for simple time-series charts. Lighter than D3 for standard chart types.
All component rendering, state management, routing between dashboards
Component model fits the role-based dashboard architecture. Concurrent features for smoother updates.
Global state management (zones, alerts, auth, UI state)
Lighter than Redux, simpler than Context for real-time data that updates frequently.
Utility styling for layout, spacing, responsive grid
Rapid iteration. CSS variables handle the color system. Purge removes unused styles.
Map renders 100s of zone polygons + nodes
WebGL via Mapbox GL JS handles this natively. Use GeoJSON sources with Mapbox expression-based styling — avoids per-feature React renders.
Never put Mapbox layers in React state. Manage via map.getSource().setData() for updates.
Debounce store updates. Batch multiple zone updates from single WS message before triggering re-render.
Use Zustand's batch update pattern. Only re-render components that subscribe to changed zone IDs.
D3 force simulation runs off React render cycle. Canvas renderer (not SVG) for > 50 nodes.
Use d3.select() on a canvas element. React only controls the container div dimensions.
requestAnimationFrame for scrubber playback. Pre-compute all time steps at load.
Don't use setInterval for animation. Use rAF loop with timestamp delta for consistent frame rate.
Use Mapbox raster tile source (MBTiles) rather than raw GeoJSON. Pre-tile on backend.
Convert SRTM + flood prediction to MBTiles format using tippecanoe or Mapbox CLI. Serve as static tiles.
Public portal loads zero map/D3/D3 — only a simple Mapbox embed with one zone visible.
Code-split the public portal as a separate chunk. Heavy visualization libraries not bundled into public view.
Every alert has both a color AND a text label (RED WARNING, AMBER WATCH). Map zones have color + zone name label. Never rely on color alone.
All text on dark backgrounds: #E8F4FF on #0A1628 = 10.1:1 ✓. Alert text: #FF3B5C on #0F1F35 = 4.8:1 ✓. Check with WebAIM Contrast Checker.
All interactive elements (zone click, layer toggle, alert expand, gate recommendation) reachable via Tab. Focus rings visible (blue outline).
ARIA labels on all icon buttons. Alert feed items use role='alert' for live announcements. Map has descriptive aria-label.
Add a toggle in settings that switches flood depth gradient from blue to orange-purple — safe for deuteranopia/protanopia. Alert levels also show icons (🔴🟡🟢 replaced with ✗ ! ✓ symbols).
Layout tested at 150% browser zoom. No horizontal overflow. All cards reflow gracefully.
Command Center — all zones, all alerts
Reservoir Dashboard — gauge, gate rec, upstream chart
Field Operations — tactical map, road status, staging
│ │ ├── MapLayers.js # Layer definitions (flood depth, zones, etc)
CascadeNet Frontend Design Specification v1.0 — For Internal Engineering Use
